mentions.note: Cheapest big bank at 12x earnings, buying back stock aggressively, and positioned to benefit from expected rate cuts via the Discover network.
Continues 2025 momentum as bank price-to-earnings multiples expand off historically depressed levels.
Recommended previously by Cramer; holding at current levels; caller asked about regional bank positioning.
Hammered by credit card rate cap fears that have now faded; plenty of cash; focus on humongous buyback and Discover card network strategy could be gigantic.

Q4 miss on reserve charges but credit metrics solid (charge-offs down); Brex $5.15B fintech acquisition strategic (take on Amex corporate card). Down 16% from highs at 11x earnings vs Amex 21x — at 16x multiple worth $330. Building full-service financial powerhouse.

Mixed quarter (revenue beat, EPS miss) and $5B Brex acquisition caused 16% decline. Executing on full-service financial services strategy; valuation at 11x earnings offers opportunity if closes gap to 16x.
Pulled back nicely from $259 to $225; financial sector strength continues; preferred over Synchrony.
Solid credit card company positioned to benefit as market recovers from AI-disruption fears.
Concerns about interest-rate cap threat and AI credit disruption overblown; Fairbank's Discover integration is true value driver; credit fundamentals strong; wild trader—Cramer bought weakness back.
Caller asked about PayPal; Cramer preferred Capital One Financial as better alternative stock in financial services.
At 9x earnings with minimal private credit exposure; good entry level despite horrible chart; continue owning if already in position.
Absolute favorite bank stock; buyback capability, Discover merger finally correct, acquisition headwinds from Trump cap gains reversal, strong acquisition pipeline, own it.
Credit card issuer down 1.66% but down 80 points from highs; many consumers paying very high rates; credit stress evident.
Bank rally participant; benefiting from tame delinquencies and strong consumer demand; higher rate environment helps credit card economics.
Same charge-down as American Express; credit card sector experiencing similar compression; not a concern.
